does: This paper introduces ART, an automatic red-teaming framework designed to systematically identify vulnerabilities in text-to-image generative models. Rather than relying on manually crafted adversarial prompts, ART leverages both vision-language models and large language models to establish connections between unsafe image generations and their corresponding text prompts. The framework automatically discovers prompts that cause models to generate harmful content, revealing previously unknown failure modes in popular open-source text-to-image systems. The authors conduct comprehensive experiments demonstrating that ART is effective, adaptable across different models, and produces diverse safety test cases that expose genuine vulnerabilities in current safeguards.
connects: This work builds on the growing body of research in AI safety focused on evaluating and protecting large-scale generative models. While prior work has primarily concentrated on jailbreaks and adversarial attacks against language models, this paper extends safety evaluation to the vision domain, specifically text-to-image synthesis. It responds to recent findings showing that seemingly benign prompts can unexpectedly trigger unsafe generations, moving beyond traditional adversarial attack paradigms. The paper combines insights from vision-language model safety and language model robustness research, establishing new connections between these previously separate evaluation domains and addressing the gap where text-to-image model safety remains less thoroughly studied than LLM safety.
enables: This framework enables more robust safety evaluation and improvement of text-to-image models by providing systematic methods to discover edge cases and vulnerabilities before deployment. The work supports the development of better safeguards tailored specifically to multimodal generative systems rather than direct adaptations from language-only models. By identifying diverse failure modes automatically, ART enables researchers to understand failure patterns at scale and design targeted mitigations. The approach also sets up future work on automated defense mechanisms against discovered vulnerabilities and provides a foundation for continuous safety monitoring of generative models as they evolve. The methodology could extend to other multimodal systems and establish best practices for systematic safety evaluation in generative AI development.
